what_it_does: Implements a ui:// address scheme for addressable surface elements: parsing and validation, stamping DOM refs, a tiny locus pub/sub, a single navigation sink that highlights and scrolls to elements, a capture-phase click router, and a ui:point custom-event bridge for programmatic or V pointing.
observations: Grammar is ui:// + one or more slash-delimited segments + optional trailing @state; malformed addresses return a typed error result.
resolveUiTarget is the single sink for all address-driven navigation and supports a transient flag to distinguish click navigation from voice/gesture attention.
indicate clears previous .ui-indicated highlights, applies the class to the matching data-ui-ref element, and updates the locus store.
The locus store is a hand-rolled pub/sub backed by a module-level Set of callbacks.
installAddressCapture listens in capture phase and only routes full ui:// refs without preventDefault or stopPropagation.
installPointerBridge listens for ui:point custom events and routes them to the same sink with transient: true.
cssEscape is minimal, escaping only double quotes and backslashes.
contextAt is a thin wrapper around fetchContext.
standouts: The transient vs persistent highlight distinction is encoded as a single option on the same sink, coupling UX semantics to one resolver.
A custom-event bridge (ui:point) lets external agents direct attention without duplicating highlight logic.
Comments embed many external mandate references (L10, §A, §H#5, DNA + fork, plum-vs-gold) that are not explained in-file.
conventions: Fail-loud: malformed addresses surface a Notice rather than silently returning false.
Single-sink routing: all navigation paths converge on resolveUiTarget.
Addressable elements carry the literal ui:// string in data-ui-ref and are expected to pair it with a Motion layoutId.
Capture-phase click listener is additive and non-interfering.
concerns: cssEscape is incomplete for CSS attribute selectors; characters like [, ], :, ., # are not escaped, which could break selectors for certain addresses.
setLocus mutates shared module state and is not exported, so callers cannot easily reset or inspect subscription lifecycles.
parseUiAddress uses rest.replace(/\/?@.*$/, '') after already slicing state, which is redundant but harmless.
No validation of segment contents beyond non-empty; arbitrary characters are allowed.
The ui:point bridge treats non-ui:// addresses as honest no-ops, so malformed brain addresses are silently ignored rather than failing loud.
notes: This is the surface-side address router: it owns the ui:// grammar, the single navigation sink, and the dual-mode highlight semantics for clicks versus transient pointing.
questions: Where is the corresponding backend contracts/ui_info.py:parse_ui_address and how is grammar parity maintained?
What consumes subscribeLocus and how is the notice rendered?
Does fetchContext accept any ui:// address or only specific forms?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide the one permitted address inheritance and a single navigation sink; the file does that and adds the locus store and pointer bridge, which extend but do not contradict the stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for address resolution.
Semantic distinction between selection and attention.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to unify every form of surface navigation (click, voice, gesture, brain emit) through one address resolver while preserving UX nuance: a clicked element stays selected, but a pointed element only glows briefly.
novelty: The transient flag collapses two spotlight behaviors into one sink, and the ui:point custom-event bridge lets a remote brain or RHM direct attention without duplicating highlight logic.
contribution: It contributes a self-contained address grammar, a deterministic single-sink resolver, a minimal locus pub/sub, and a custom-event bridge that lets programmatic callers reuse the same highlight and scroll behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript module that parses ui:// addresses, stamps DOM references, maintains a locus store, and provides a single sink plus capture-phase click and ui:point custom-event bridges to highlight and scroll to addressable surface elements.
intention_for_embedding: To make every addressable UI element reachable through one shared resolver, distinguishing persistent selection from transient attention, and to let external agents point at surface elements without adding a second spotlight path.

symbols.does: Parses and validates a ui:// address into segments and an optional state.
symbols.description: Returns a discriminated result with either the parsed segments and state or a reason string; mirrors a backend grammar and is the foundation for all address validation in the file.

symbols.does: Type guard that checks whether a value is a valid ui:// address string.
symbols.description: Wraps parseUiAddress to provide a boolean and type-narrowing check for nullable inputs.

symbols.does: Returns the data-ui-ref DOM attribute object for a literal address.
symbols.description: Encodes the convention that addressable elements must carry the full ui:// string in data-ui-ref and are intended to pair it with a Motion layoutId in JSX.

symbols.does: Type describing the current indicated address and any active notice.
symbols.description: Defines the shape of the module-level pub/sub state: an optional address and an optional human-readable notice.
Provides read access to the module's mutable _locus object.

symbols.does: Registers a callback for locus changes and returns an unsubscribe function.
symbols.description: Implements the pub/sub surface so React or other consumers can react to address and notice updates.

symbols.does: Updates the module-level locus and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: Mutates _locus and broadcasts the new value to every registered subscriber.

symbols.does: Marks an element as indicated and updates the locus store.
symbols.description: Clears previous .ui-indicated highlights, applies the class to the matching data-ui-ref element, and records the address in the locus.

symbols.does: Single sink that validates, indicates, spotlights, and scrolls to a ui:// address.
symbols.description: Handles all address-driven navigation; malformed addresses emit a notice, valid addresses receive a transient glow and smooth scroll, and the transient option controls whether the persistent .ui-indicated highlight remains.
Convenience update that sets the notice field to null without changing the current address.

symbols.does: Fetches a context bundle for a given address.
symbols.description: Thin async wrapper around fetchContext, allowing callers to retrieve backend context associated with a ui:// address.

symbols.does: Installs a capture-phase click listener that routes data-ui-ref clicks to resolveUiTarget.
symbols.description: Only full ui:// refs are processed; the listener is additive and does not interfere with normal event propagation.

symbols.does: Minimal escaping for attribute selector string values.
symbols.description: Escapes only double quotes and backslashes; used to safely quote a ui:// address inside a CSS attribute selector.

symbols.does: Listens for ui:point custom events and routes them to resolveUiTarget as transient pointing.
symbols.description: Provides the programmatic twin to click capture, allowing external agents to spotlight surface elements without duplicating highlight logic.
